rules.name: no-legacy-svelte-reactive-statements-edit
rules.evidence: Follow existing Svelte 5 runes patterns (no legacy reactive statements)

rules.name: no-legacy-svelte-reactive-statements-write
rules.evidence: Follow existing Svelte 5 runes patterns (no legacy reactive statements)

uncompilable: Deployment reminder to run build+deploy 'when you are done making changes' — no discrete tool-call trigger, applies to session end rather than a specific action
Use TypeScript for all components with proper type imports — general style guidance, no detectable violation pattern
Maintain consistent styling with Tailwind utility classes — general style guidance, not action-bindable
Component props should be properly typed interfaces — general style guidance
API responses follow APIResponse<T> interface / proper HTTP status codes / CORS handling / multipart form-data validation — documentation-level API conventions without a concrete detectable pattern
State management bullets (Svelte stores, Supabase auth sync) — documentation of existing architecture, not an obligation
All new features should have Playwright E2E tests — feature-level requirement with no specific file/tool trigger
Use authenticated user state for tests requiring login — general testing guidance, not action-bindable
After feature implementation, run tests to verify functionality — broad workflow guidance, trigger too broad to bind
Development Workflow numbered steps (make changes → test → typecheck → lint → deploy → verify) — general process documentation, not a discrete action-bound rule
Update DEVELOPMENT.md task status if applicable — vague conditional, no concrete trigger
Commands tables (Development/Code Quality/Testing/Deployment sections) — usage catalogs listing what you can run, no obligation or prohibition attached
